metadata.seed: Yeah, last week I was on my way to class and my girlfriend actually called me up saying that I forgot to take out the trash. And I kind of started thinking, wow, like, I forgot to take out the trash. Something so simple, a simple chore. All I had to do was take out the trash today and I forgot to do it. And then I started to think, well, I can't do something as simple as that. Why is she still with me? Or, and why, why can't I do that? Why can't I do a simple task? Am I good enough for anyone? Am I good enough for her? Kind of starting to feel a little fearful that she would just up and leave me.

patient.query: Yeah, last week I was on my way to class and my girlfriend actually called me up saying that I forgot to take out the trash. And I kind of started thinking, wow, like, I forgot to take out the trash. Something so simple, a simple chore. All I had to do was take out the trash today and I forgot to do it. And then I started to think, well, I can't do something as simple as that. Why is she still with me? Or, and why, why can't I do that? Why can't I do a simple task? Am I good enough for anyone? Am I good enough for her? Kind of starting to feel a little fearful that she would just up and leave me.

triggers: Receiving a call from girlfriend about forgetting to take out the trash
automatic_thoughts: I forgot to take out the trash, something so simple.
I can't do something as simple as that.
Why is she still with me?
Am I good enough for anyone?
Am I good enough for her?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I just felt... really disappointed in myself. Like, it was such a small thing, but it felt like it meant something bigger about me. I started worrying that if I can't even handle this little task, how can I be good enough for anyone else? It’s like I just jumped to thinking they must see me as unreliable or something.

I can't do something as simple as that.
Why is she still with me?
Am I good enough for anyone?
Am I good enough for her?
If I can't even handle this little task, how can I be good enough for anyone else?
They must see me as unreliable.

patient.query: It feels pretty heavy, honestly. Like this weight just sitting on my chest. I start imagining what they might be thinking, and it makes me feel so vulnerable. It’s like I want to just disappear because I worry they'll see me as a mess or not worth their time.

I can't do something as simple as that.
Why is she still with me?

They must see me as unreliable.
I worry they'll see me as a mess or not worth their time.

patient.query: I don't even know... it seems nice to think about. But then I just picture myself being judged or laughed at for slipping up. There’s this voice in my head that keeps reminding me of all the times I messed up before. Letting go feels... scary, I guess? Like, what if I let my guard down and it just gets worse?

Like, what if I let myself relax a bit and it just allows everything to fall apart? Then I’d have to deal with even more fallout.

patient.query: I think stepping off would be... a relief, but also terrifying. It’s like I want to breathe easy, but I’m so used to being on edge that it feels wrong somehow. Part of me is scared that if I let go, I’ll just fall into chaos or lose the little bit of control I have. But another part of me wonders what it would be like to just... be. To not feel like I have to constantly monitor everything.

patient.query: I think it would feel... weird, honestly. Like, maybe freeing, but also like I’d be stepping into unknown territory. There’s a part of me that’s curious about what it would be like to just trust things to unfold. But then there’s this fear bubbling up about losing control, like if I do that, everything will start to unravel, and I’ll be left trying to pick up the pieces again. It’s a bit of a rollercoaster in my mind right now.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is a mix. I think what's pressing the most is the fear. I feel like it’s this constant shadow that reminds me of past failures—like those moments where I let my guard down and something went wrong. It just feels so heavy, like it could crush any little spark of curiosity I have. Sometimes I wonder if it’s even worth it to try and explore that curiosity when fear feels so much more real.

patient.query: Honestly, I think it would feel... bright? Like, maybe there’s a little bit of hope there, a chance to breathe without that weight pressing down. But then, I also feel this instinct to pull back, like I need to protect myself from the unknown. It’s just so hard to imagine being truly free of that fear for even a moment. I worry about what that would mean—would I still be okay? Would I just fall back into old patterns?
